headline: Claude Code feature faces mounting criticism over usability and cost control
body: Anthropic's Claude Code feature is drawing significant developer backlash over functionality issues and runaway spending risks, with multiple reports of organizations losing thousands due to uncapped API usage. A detailed technical critique frames the feature as fundamentally misdesigned, while users report Fable functionality disappearing behind credit paywalls and vague cost guardrails. For healthcare vendors building AI-assisted clinical workflows or documentation tools, this signals the importance of implementing strict token budgets and usage monitoring when integrating LLM features into production systems. The spending and reliability concerns highlight why healthcare organizations should architect clear cost controls and fallback mechanisms before deploying Claude or similar models in high-volume EHR integration scenarios. This pattern suggests vendors need tighter vendor management of third-party model providers to prevent unexpected cost overruns that could undermine value-based care economics.
